explanations.2: This runs the sqlplus database tool with -S (silent mode) to connect to an Oracle database using sys credentials, and the <<EOF syntax lets us pass multiple SQL commands to it without creating a separate file.
explanations.4: This SQL command switches the database session to work with a specific pluggable database container named ORCLPDB1 instead of the default one.
explanations.6: This reconnects to the database with different credentials (system user) targeting the specific pluggable database, so subsequent commands run as that user in that database.
